title: DBISAM engine verdict
description: Per-entry cached output from the engine harness. Schema follows CORPUS.md §'engine_verdict.json'. Captured by corpus-refresh-verdicts (not yet built) against the live engine via dbisam-dcg-project/harness/engine.

engine_version.description: The DBISAM engine version string. 'unknown' is accepted in the seed corpus until version probing is added.

error_code.description: Engine error code, e.g. from a DBISAM Engine Error #NNNN message. Distinct from the wire reqcode (which is captured in harness_reqcode while error_code decoding is not yet implemented; see task #7).

harness_reqcode.description: The DBISAM wire reqcode from the response body header (e.g. '0x2b02' for table-not-found, '0x2ead' for parse error). See ../../docs/reqcodes.md for the catalogue.

harness_response_bytes.description: Size of the raw engine response, in bytes. Coarse sanity check, not authoritative.

harness_table.description: 0x2b02 only: the offending table name decoded from the rejection Pack stream.

harness_catalog.description: 0x2ead only: the database/catalog scope the engine reports the error against (e.g. 'NISAINT_CS').

harness_code_a.description: 0x2ead only: first numeric code from the rejection Pack stream. Empirically appears to be an error-class indicator (1 = expected-X-found-Y, 2 = missing-required-keyword) but the mapping is provisional.

harness_code_b.description: 0x2ead only: second numeric code from the rejection Pack stream. Empirically appears to be a 1-based character position in the input SQL — verified against `select FOO from CUSTOMER` where code_b=8 lines up with the start byte of 'FOO'.
